description_long: This is a hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle with a natural grain pattern and a smooth finish. The head of the axe is made of metal, with a broad, rectangular blade that has a textured, matte surface, likely for increased grip and durability. The blade is single-edged, with a flat poll on the opposite side, suitable for striking or hammering. The handle is securely fitted into the eye of the blade, suggesting a traditional wedged mounting style. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements or branding. This axe is typical of those found in urban garages, workshops, or maintenance sheds for chopping wood or general utility tasks.
description: A sturdy hand axe with a wooden handle and a broad, textured metal blade, designed for chopping and utility work. The handle is slightly curved and the blade is single-edged with a flat poll.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the wooden handle and the broad, textured metal blade.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the narrow edge of the blade and the alignment of the handle within the blade's eye.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the handle's curvature and the flat poll of the axe head.
description_view_3: End-on view of the handle, showing its rounded cross-section and the top of the blade.
attribute_handle_type: Slightly curved wooden handle with a smooth finish and rounded cross-section, designed for ergonomic grip.
attribute_blade_design: Broad, rectangular single-edged steel blade with a flat poll and textured surface; no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the handle fitted through the eye of the blade.
